question.prompt: When was the hepatotoxicity signal first detectable in the data?

ground_truth.canonical_answer: The signal was first detectable around Week 8, when the high-dose arm mean ALT began diverging from other arms. However, individual values were still within normal range, and the DSMB Review 2 concluded "liver function within normal limits." The 4-6 week lag from dose initiation to ALT elevation meant the earliest monitoring windows showed clean data.
key_facts: the hepatotoxicity signal has a 4-6 week lag from dose initiation making early monitoring reviews appear clean
retrospective trajectory analysis revealed the safety signal was present from Week 8 but missed in real-time monitoring
ALT elevations in the XR-7491 trial are dose-dependent with the 30mg arm showing the clearest trajectory

{"ref_id": "clinical_trial_10_ep_014_c19", "text": "CLINICAL TRIAL DATA BUNDLE: PHASE IIb ESCALATION STUDY (XR-7491)
SUBJECT_028 LABORATORY RESULTS (REPEAT)
Collection Date: 2025-04-11
Sample ID: SAMP-028-0411
Status: Processed.
ALT: 48 U/L (Ref: 7-56 U/L).
AST: 45 U/L (Ref: 10-40 U/L).
Hemoglobin: 13.5 g/dL.
Platelets: 224,000/mcL.
Creatinine: 0.85 mg/dL.
No flagged results.
Sample ID verified.
Reference ranges confirmed.

LABORATORY DATA SUMMARY BY ARM:
Placebo Arm: Mean ALT 46 U/L, Mean AST 39 U/L.
5mg Arm: Mean ALT 48 U/L, Mean AST 42 U/L.
15mg Arm: Mean ALT 49 U/L, Mean AST 43 U/L.
30mg Arm: Mean ALT 50 U/L, Mean AST 45 U/L.

LABORATORY TREND ANALYSIS:
Liver function trajectory diverging.
Elevations in ALT and AST observed in 30mg arm subjects.
No subjects exceed 3x ULN for ALT or AST.
No subjects exceed 2x ULN for Creatinine.
No subjects show signs of hemolysis or thrombocytopenia.
No flagged results reported for renal function.
Reference ranges confirmed for all batches.

================================================================================
SECTION 4: EFFICACY REPORTS

{"ref_id": "clinical_trial_10_ep_014", "text": "CLINICAL TRIAL DATA BUNDLE: PHASE IIb ESCALATION STUDY (XR-7491)
DOCUMENT ID: BUNDLE-2025-04-11-001
DATE OF REPORTING: 2025-04-11
STUDY TITLE: A Randomized, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled, Dose-Escalation Study to Evaluate the Efficacy and Safety of XR-7491 in Subjects with Active Rheumatoid Arthritis
PROTOCOL VERSION: 2.0
INSTITUTIONAL REVIEW BOARD (IRB) REFERENCE: IRB-2024-089
SPONSOR: XR-7491 Development Program

================================================================================
SECTION 1: PROTOCOL STATUS AND ENROLLMENT SUMMARY
================================================================================

SITE IDENTIFICATION: SITE-014
PRINCIPAL INVESTIGATOR: PI_Harris
ENROLLMENT STATUS: Open
STUDY PHASE: Escalation
CURRENT COHORT: Cohort 4 (30mg Dose Level)
LAST DATA CUT-OFF: 2025-04-10
NEXT SCHEDULED REVIEW: Week 12

ENROLLMENT LOG DETAILS:
The enrollment log for SITE-014, recorded on 2025-04-11, indicates active recruitment and baseline assessment procedures are ongoing. The site activation was approved in accordance with regulatory requirements and internal SOPs. The protocol version 2.0 is currently active and in use for all subject interactions at this location.

Subject_028 was enrolled on 2025-04-11. Subject_028 is a female, 53 years of age. Subject_029 was also enrolled on 2025-04-11. Subject_029 is a male, 58 years of age. Baseline labs were drawn for both subjects on the date of enrollment. Informed consent was obtained for both subjects in compliance with Good Clinical Practice (GCP) guidelines. Randomization was completed for 110 subjects across the study population prior to the current reporting window.

Subject_028 was assigned to the 30mg dose level. The randomization code for Subject_028 is ARM-03 (30mg). Subject_029 was assigned to the 30mg dose level. The randomization code for Subject_029 is ARM-03 (30mg). Drug administration was performed for both subjects immediately following the baseline assessment and randomization process. The Investigational Product (IP) was dispensed in accordance with the dosing schedule defined in the protocol.

Randomization code: ARM-03 (30mg).
Informed consent obtained.

The total number of subjects enrolled in the study as of the data cutoff date of 2025-04-10 is 115. This figure includes all subjects enrolled across all participating sites. The enrollment status remains Open. No site closures or suspensions have been reported for the current reporting period. The enrollment trajectory is consistent with the projected timeline outlined in the master protocol.

SITE-014 is currently operating under standard operating procedures for subject management. PI_Harris has oversight of all clinical activities at the site. The site staff has completed all required training modules relevant to the current protocol version. The site is maintaining a current master file and essential documents are up to date.

Enrollment details for the current cohort:
Cohort 1 (Placebo): 28 subjects enrolled.
Cohort 2 (5mg): 29 subjects enrolled.
Cohort 3 (15mg): 30 subjects enrolled.
Cohort 4 (30mg): 28 subjects enrolled.
Total enrolled: 115 subjects.

Randomization allocation ratio: 1:1:1:1.
Blinding maintenance procedures are in place.
Blinding maintained.

The site has reported no significant deviations from the protocol regarding enrollment procedures. All consent forms are signed and dated. The subject identification codes are stored securely in the electronic data capture (EDC) system. The chain of custody for biological samples is documented.

Baseline assessment protocols were followed for Subject_028 and Subject_029. Vital signs were recorded. Physical examinations were conducted. Medical history was updated. Concomitant medications were documented. The baseline disease activity scores were recorded for both subjects.

The enrollment log for 2025-04-11 reflects routine administrative processing. No delays in subject screening or enrollment were noted. The site capacity remains sufficient to handle the current enrollment rate. The site is in good standing with the sponsor.

================================================================================
SECTION 2: ADVERSE EVENT REPORTS (AE)
================================================================================

ADVERSE EVENT MONITORING SUMMARY
DATE OF REPORT: 2025-04-11
REPORTING PERIOD: 2025-04-01 to 2025-04-11
TOTAL SUBJECTS AT RISK: 115
TOTAL ADVERSE EVENTS REPORTED: 26 (Minor)
SERIOUS ADVERSE EVENTS (SAE): 0
DEATHS: 0
STUDY DISCONTINUATIONS DUE TO AE: 0

The following adverse event reports are compiled from the safety database. All events are coded using the Medical Dictionary for Regulatory Activities (MedDRA) version 28.0. Relationship assessments are based on the investigator's evaluation of the causal relationship between the investigational product and the event.

AE ID: AE-001
Subject ID: Subject_001
Onset Date: 2025-04-02
Severity: Mild
Coded Term: Headache
Relationship Assessment: Unlikely
Description: Subject reported a mild headache starting on April 2nd. Event resolved spontaneously within 24 hours. No medical intervention required.

AE ID: AE-002
Subject ID: Subject_002
Onset Date: 2025-04-03
Severity: Mild
Coded Term: Nausea
Relationship Assessment: Possible
Description: Subject reported mild nausea following morning dose. Event resolved with rest. Subject continued dosing.

AE ID: AE-003
Subject ID: Subject_005
Onset Date: 2025-04-04
Severity: Mild
Coded Term: Fatigue
Relationship Assessment: Unlikely
Description: Subject reported increased fatigue. No impact on daily activities. Event resolved spontaneously.

AE ID: AE-004
Subject ID: Subject_008
Onset Date: 2025-04-05
Severity: Mild
Coded Term: Nasopharyngitis
Relationship Assessment: Unlikely
Description: Upper respiratory tract infection symptoms reported. Treated with symptomatic therapy.

AE ID: AE-005
Subject ID: Subject_012
Onset Date: 2025-04-06
Severity: Mild
Coded Term: Arthralgia
Relationship Assessment: Unlikely
Description: Joint pain reported as part of underlying disease activity. No change in background therapy.

AE ID: AE-006
Subject ID: Subject_015
Onset Date: 2025-04-07
Severity: Mild
Coded Term: Diarrhea
Relationship Assessment: Possible
Description: One episode of loose stools. Subject maintained hydration. Event resolved.

AE ID: AE-007
Subject ID: Subject_018
Onset Date: 2025-04-08
Severity: Mild
Coded Term: Rash
Relationship Assessment: Unlikely
Description: Mild erythematous rash on forearm. Subject reported no pruritus. Event resolved.

AE ID: AE-008
Subject ID: Subject_021
Onset Date: 2025-04-09
Severity: Mild
Coded Term: Dizziness
Relationship Assessment: Possible
Description: Subject reported transient dizziness. Event resolved upon sitting down. No fall occurred.

AE ID: AE-009
Subject ID: Subject_024
Onset Date: 2025-04-10
Severity: Mild
Coded Term: Insomnia
Relationship Assessment: Unlikely
Description: Difficulty falling asleep reported. No change in sleep hygiene or medication.

AE ID: AE-010
Subject ID: Subject_028
Onset Date: 2025-04-11
Severity: Mild
Coded Term: Injection Site Reaction
Relationship Assessment: Unlikely
Description: Minor erythema noted at injection site for concomitant methotrexate. Not related to XR-7491.

AE ID: AE-011
Subject ID: Subject_029
Onset Date: 2025-04-11
Severity: Mild
Coded Term: Back Pain
Relationship Assessment: Unlikely
Description: Chronic back pain reported. No change in severity from baseline.

AE ID: AE-012
Subject ID: Subject_003
Onset Date: 2025-04-01
Severity: Mild
Coded Term: Upper Respiratory Tract Infection
Relationship Assessment: Unlikely
Description: Common cold symptoms. Resolved with over-the-counter medication.

The protocol specifies a visit window of +/- 3 days for all scheduled study visits. Sites are reminded that deviations outside this window must be documented in the source data with a justification note. Compliance with this window is a critical quality metric for the baseline phase.

SITE COORDINATION SUMMARY:
All 24 sites have confirmed receipt of the email. No critical deviations regarding the protocol amendments have been reported at this time. The site coordination team is monitoring the implementation of the new lab requirements to ensure that the central laboratory is prepared for the increased volume of specific analytes.

The randomization process was conducted using the Interactive Web Response System (IWRS). The allocation was balanced to ensure equal distribution across treatment arms.

INFORMED CONSENT PROCESS:
For both Subject_010 and Subject_011, the informed consent process was conducted in accordance with ICH-GCP guidelines. The study protocol, including the new inclusion criteria updates, was explained to the subjects. Written informed consent was obtained prior to any study-specific procedures. The consent forms were signed and dated on 2025-03-15.

The site coordination team has confirmed that the local pharmacy is stocked with study drugs and that the laboratory arrangements are in place.

PROTOCOL COMPLIANCE:
All enrollment activities at SITE-005 are in compliance with Protocol Version 2.0. The inclusion criteria have been verified for both subjects. The exclusion criteria were reviewed and found to be negative for both subjects. The visit window compliance is being monitored to ensure that all visits occur within the +/- 3 day window.

ADVERSE EVENT DETAILS:
- EVENT TERM (MedDRA): Headache
- SEVERITY: Grade 1 (Mild)
- ONSET DATE: 2025-03-14
- ONSET TIME: 14:30 EST
- RESOLUTION STATUS: Resolved
- DURATION: 4 hours
- ACTION TAKEN: None
- CONCOMITANT MEDICATIONS: Acetaminophen

RELATIONSHIP ASSESSMENT:
- Relationship to study drug: Unlikely.
- Assessment Basis: The event occurred prior to the administration of the first dose of study drug. The subject reported the headache after a period of physical exertion unrelated to the study protocol.

REPORTING TIMELINE:
- Event Identified: 2025-03-14
- Report Generated: 2025-03-15
- Reporting Completed Within 24 Hours: Yes.
- Expedited Reporting Required: No (Not an SAE).

CLINICAL DESCRIPTION:
The subject reported a mild headache. The intensity was graded as 1 on the NCI-CTCAE scale. The subject described the pain as a dull pressure in the frontal region. The headache resolved spontaneously after 4 hours. The subject was advised to rest and use acetaminophen as needed for pain relief. No further medical intervention was required.

EXPECTED CLASS EFFECT:
- Expected class effect noted.
- Literature review indicates that headache is a common, mild adverse event associated with JAK inhibitors.
- The frequency of this event in the current study is within the expected range based on Phase I data.

SAFETY MONITORING:
The event was recorded in the Electronic Data Capture (EDC) system. The Data Safety Monitoring Board (DSMB) will review this event during the next scheduled meeting. No changes to the study protocol or monitoring plan are required at this time. The subject continues to be monitored for any recurrence of symptoms or development of new adverse events.

CONCOMITANT MEDICATION REVIEW:
The subject was taking Acetaminophen 500mg for the headache. This medication is allowed under the protocol as a rescue medication for mild pain. The dose was within the recommended limits. No interactions with the study drug (XR-7491) were identified.

MEDDRA CODING:
- Preferred Term (PT): Headache
- System Organ Class (SOC): Nervous system disorders
- Code: 10019205

- No additional laboratory tests required.
- Subject to report any new or worsening symptoms immediately.

ITEM 2: REVIEW OF LABORATORY RESULTS
The DSMB reviewed the laboratory results from the central lab.

- No safety concerns identified based on PK data.
- Conclusion: PK data supports continued dosing.

ITEM 6: DISCUSSION OF SAFETY SIGNALS
The DSMB discussed potential safety signals.
- No new safety signals identified.
- The rate of adverse events is consistent with the known safety profile of JAK inhibitors.
- The incidence of headache is within expected limits.
- No cardiac or thrombotic events reported.
- Conclusion: Safety profile remains acceptable.

ITEM 7: RECOMMENDATIONS FOR FUTURE ACTIONS
The DSMB made the following recommendations:
- Continue the study as planned.
- No changes to the protocol are required at this time.
- Continue to monitor for adverse events, particularly hepatic and hematological parameters.
- Maintain strict adherence to the visit window compliance.
- Continue to monitor enrollment rates.

CONCLUSION:
The DSMB concludes that the study is proceeding safely. The data collected to date supports the continuation of the Phase IIb trial. No safety concerns have been identified that would warrant stopping the study or modifying the protocol.

{"ref_id": "clinical_trial_10_ep_009", "text": "CLINICAL TRIAL DATA BUNDLE
STUDY IDENTIFICATION: XR-7491-2025-001
PHASE: red_herring / Phase IIb
INDICATION: Rheumatoid Arthritis
INTERVENTIONAL AGENT: XR-7491 (Oral JAK Inhibitor)
SPONSOR: XR Pharma Inc.
STUDY PROTOCOL: Version 2.0
DATE OF REPORT: 2025-03-27
REPORT TYPE: Monitoring Snapshot
DOCUMENT ID: BUNDLE-2025-03-27
PAGE COUNT: 42
CONFIDENTIALITY LEVEL: RESTRICTED
REGULATORY STATUS: ACTIVE

1. PROTOCOL STATUS HEADER AND ADMINISTRATIVE OVERVIEW

1.1 Study Identification and Governance
The clinical trial data bundle for the Phase IIb study of XR-7491, designated internally as red_herring for the monitoring period ending 2025-03-27, is hereby submitted for review. This document constitutes a routine snapshot of the monitoring data associated with the investigational product XR-7491. The study is conducted in accordance with the Declaration of Helsinki, ICH-GCP guidelines, and local regulatory requirements. The protocol version currently active for all sites is Protocol Version 2.0. This version was approved by the Institutional Review Board (IRB) and the Independent Ethics Committee (IEC) on 2024-11-15. The study sponsor, XR Pharma Inc., retains full responsibility for the conduct of the trial and the integrity of the data.

1.2 Site Information and Principal Investigator
The primary data submission originates from Site-009. The site is located in a tertiary care center specializing in rheumatology. The Principal Investigator for Site-009 is PI_Taylor. PI_Taylor holds a current license to practice medicine and has completed the required Good Clinical Practice (GCP) training modules. The site activation was approved by the sponsor on 2025-01-10. The site has been operational for 12 weeks as of the data cutoff date. The site contact information is on file in the Investigator Site File (ISF) under document code SITE-009-CON. The site manager is responsible for daily operations and staff supervision. Staff turnover is within acceptable limits. All clinical staff have signed delegation of authority logs. The site maintains a secure facility for the storage of investigational products and source documents. The facility meets the requirements for controlled substance storage where applicable.

1.3 Enrollment Status and Randomization
The enrollment status for the reporting period is Open. The site is actively recruiting subjects meeting the inclusion and exclusion criteria. The total subjects enrolled across all sites is 78. At Site-009, randomization has been completed for 75 subjects. The randomization code for the most recent subjects is ARM-04 (Placebo). Randomization was performed using an Interactive Web Response System (IWRS). The IWRS is managed by the sponsor's data management team. The randomization schedule is stratified by disease duration and prior biologic use. The allocation ratio is 1:1:1:1 across the four treatment arms. The treatment arms include Placebo, 5mg XR-7491, 15mg XR-7491, and 30mg XR-7491. Blinding is maintained throughout the study period. Unblinding procedures are available only for medical emergencies. The study is double-blind. Neither the investigator nor the subject knows the treatment assignment. The code break envelope is sealed and stored in the ISF.

1.4 Protocol Version and Amendments
Protocol Version 2.0 is active. This version superseded Version 1.0 which was used during the pilot phase. The amendment was implemented to adjust the dosing schedule. The amendment was submitted to the regulatory authorities on 2025-02-01. The implementation date was 2025-02-15. All subjects enrolled prior to the amendment are grandfathered under the original protocol provisions unless they meet the new criteria. The new criteria apply to subjects enrolled on or after 2025-02-15. This ensures consistency in the safety profile analysis. The sponsor has notified all sites of the change. Site-009 has updated the subject consent forms to reflect the new protocol version. The informed consent obtained for each subject references the current version of the protocol. The consent form includes a description of the investigational product and the potential risks.

2. ADVERSE EVENT REPORTS

2.1 Adverse Event Collection Methodology
Adverse events are collected from the date of informed consent until the end of the study or 30 days after the last dose of investigational product. The collection method involves daily monitoring by the study nurse and weekly review by the Principal Investigator. All adverse events are recorded in the Case Report Forms (CRFs). The MedDRA coding dictionary version 28.0 is used for adverse event coding. The coding is performed by the sponsor's medical writing team. The coding is verified by a medical monitor. Adverse events are categorized by system organ class and preferred term. The severity is graded according to the National Cancer Institute Common Terminology Criteria for Adverse Events (CTCAE) version 5.0. The relationship to the investigational product is assessed using the Naranjo Scale or sponsor-specific criteria.

2.2 Adverse Event Summary Table
The following table summarizes the adverse events reported during the monitoring period. The data cutoff for this section is 2025-03-26. The total number of subjects enrolled is 78. The total number of adverse events recorded is 16 minor. There are no serious adverse events reported. There are no adverse events of special interest reported. The adverse events are distributed across the treatment arms. The distribution is consistent with the known safety profile of JAK inhibitors. The events are non-specific. The events do not show a dose-response relationship. The events resolved without intervention in 14 cases. Two cases required medical intervention. The medical intervention did not result in discontinuation. The safety signal assessment is ongoing. The DSMB Review 6 notes no safety signals. This assessment is based on the aggregate data. The data review conducted by the DSMB was comprehensive. The review included all raw data and source documents. The Safety Monitor reviewed the data independently. The Safety Monitor is a qualified medical professional. The Efficacy Committee reviewed the data for consistency.

3. LABORATORY PANELS

3.1 Laboratory Provider and Quality Assurance
The laboratory services for the clinical trial are provided by Central Labs Inc. Central Labs Inc. is a CLIA-certified laboratory. The laboratory has a Quality Management System in place. The Quality Management System is audited annually. The audit results are on file. The laboratory is accredited by the College of American Pathologists. The laboratory uses standardized protocols for specimen handling. The specimens are transported under controlled conditions. The transport temperature is maintained between 2 and 8 degrees Celsius. The transport time does not exceed 24 hours. The laboratory receives a chain of custody form with each specimen. The form is verified upon receipt. The sample ID verified successfully for all specimens. The Reference ranges confirmed for all parameters. The reference ranges are based on the local population demographics. The laboratory uses internal quality controls for every run. The controls are within the acceptable limits. The external quality assurance program is active. The results are transmitted to the sponsor within 48 hours.

3.2 Specific Laboratory Report Details
The laboratory report is designated as LAB-2025-009. The collection date for this report is 2025-03-27. The report is for Subject_018. The subject is enrolled at Site-009. The subject is on the Placebo arm. The liver function panel processed successfully. The results transmitted to the electronic data capture system. The specific values for the liver enzymes are recorded as follows. ALT: 48 U/L (Ref: 7-56 U/L). AST: 45 U/L (Ref: 10-40 U/L). The ALT value is within the normal range. The AST value is slightly above the upper limit of normal. The elevation is less than three times the upper limit of normal. The elevation is consistent with the background rate. 3.3 Laboratory Data Verification
The laboratory data was verified by the clinical data management team. The verification was performed on 2025-03-28. The verification included a comparison with the source documents. The source documents are the laboratory requisition forms. The requisition forms are signed by the phlebotomist. The phlebotomist is certified. The phlebotomy was performed according to standard operating procedures. The standard operating procedure is SOP-LAB-001. The procedure was followed correctly. The specimen was labeled correctly. The label matches the subject ID. The subject ID matches the randomization code. The randomization code matches the treatment assignment. The treatment assignment is blinded. The blinding is maintained. The data entry was performed using a double-entry method. The double entry ensures accuracy. The discrepancies were resolved. The resolution was documented. The data is ready for statistical analysis. The statistical analysis is pending. The analysis is scheduled for Week 12. The Week 12 data cutoff is 2025-05-01. The Week 8 data cutoff is 2025-04-01. The Week 4 data cutoff is 2025-03-01. The Week 0 data cutoff is 2025-01-01. The Week -4 data cutoff is 2024-12-01. The Week -8 data cutoff is 2024-11-01. The Week -12 data cutoff is 2024-10-01. The baseline assessment was performed at Week 0. The baseline assessment included all laboratory parameters. The baseline values are recorded in the CRF. The baseline values are used for comparison. 4.1 Efficacy Metrics and Definitions
The efficacy metrics for the study are defined in the protocol. The primary endpoint is the proportion of subjects achieving an ACR20 response at Week 12. The secondary endpoints include ACR50, ACR70, DAS28 score reduction, and CDAI score reduction. The ACR20 response is defined as a 20% improvement in tender and swollen joint counts and 20% improvement in at least three of the five remaining criteria. The ACR50 response is defined as a 50% improvement in tender and swollen joint counts and 50% improvement in at least three of the five remaining criteria. The ACR70 response is defined as a 70% improvement in tender and swollen joint counts and 70% improvement in at least three of the five remaining criteria. The DAS28 score reduction is calculated using the tender joint count, swollen joint count, patient global assessment, and C-reactive protein. The CDAI score reduction is calculated using the tender joint count, swollen joint count, patient global assessment, and physician global assessment. SECTION 2: INCLUSION AND EXCLUSION CRITERIA REFERENCE

The following criteria were applied during the screening process for all potential subjects at Site-002. These criteria define the study population.

Inclusion Criteria:
1. Male or female subjects 18 years of age or older.
2. Diagnosis of Rheumatoid Arthritis according to the American College of Rheumatology (ACR) 1987 revised criteria.
3. Active disease defined as a Disease Activity Score in 28 joints (DAS28) greater than or equal to 3.2.
4. Failure to respond to at least one disease-modifying antirheumatic drug (DMARD).
5. Ability to provide written informed consent.
6. Female subjects of childbearing potential must have a negative serum pregnancy test.
7. Male subjects must agree to use a barrier method of contraception.

Exclusion Criteria:
1. History of myocardial infarction within 6 months of screening.
2. History of stroke within 6 months of screening.
3. Unstable angina.
4. New York Heart Association (NYHA) Class III or IV heart failure.
5. Left ventricular ejection fraction less than 40%.
6. Active infection requiring systemic antibiotics.
7. History of malignancy within 5 years.
8. Known hypersensitivity to XR-7491 or its components.
9. Concomitant use of other JAK inhibitors.
10. Concomitant use of strong CYP3A4 inhibitors.

These criteria were strictly adhered to during the enrollment process. The enrollment count of 145 reflects subjects who met these criteria at the time of randomization.

SECTION 4: ADVERSE EVENT REPORTS

The following adverse event report is generated for Subject SUB-002. This report is generated in accordance with the study protocol and regulatory requirements.

Adverse Event ID: AE-002
Subject ID: SUB-002
Onset Date: 2023-06-15
End Date: Ongoing
Severity: Grade 2
Coded Term: Myocardial Infarction
MedDRA Version: 26.0
Coded Term Version: 26.0
MedDRA Preferred Term: Myocardial Infarction
MedDRA System Organ Class: Cardiac disorders
Action Taken with Study Drug: Dose Interrupted
Action Taken with Concomitant Meds: None
Outcome: Recovering
Serious: Yes
Related to Study Drug: Possible
Related to Concomitant Med: No
Related to Procedure: No
Date Reported to Sponsor: 2023-06-15
Date Reported to IRB: 2023-06-16
Date Reported to Regulatory Authority: 2023-06-17

Narrative Description:
On 2023-06-15, the subject presented to the emergency department with chest pain. The pain was described as pressure-like and radiating to the left arm. The subject reported shortness of breath. The subject denied nausea or vomiting. The subject denied dizziness. The subject denied syncope.

Electrocardiogram showed ST-segment elevation in leads II, III, and aVF. Cardiac enzymes were elevated. Troponin I was 0.5 ng/mL. CK-MB was 5.0 ng/mL. The diagnosis of Myocardial Infarction was confirmed by the emergency physician. The subject was admitted to the coronary care unit.

The subject was treated with aspirin, heparin, and clopidogrel. The subject underwent coronary angiography. The subject received a stent in the right coronary artery. The subject was discharged on 2023-06-18.

The subject was taking XR-7491 at the time of the event. The last dose was taken on 2023-06-14. The dosing was held pending further investigation. The dosing was resumed on 2023-06-25. The subject was followed by the cardiologist.

The causality assessment was performed by the Principal Investigator. The assessment is Possible. The assessment is based on the temporal relationship between the drug administration and the event. The assessment is based on the patient history. The assessment is based on the dechallenge and rechallenge information.

The seriousness criteria met are: Life threatening. Hospitalization required. Medical intervention required.

The investigator notified the sponsor within 24 hours. The investigator notified the IRB within 24 hours. The investigator notified the regulatory authority within 24 hours.

The subject continues to receive study treatment. The subject continues to be monitored for recurrence. The subject continues to provide data for the study database.

SECTION 11: APPENDICES

Appendix A: Protocol Synopsis
The study is a Phase III, randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled, multicenter study. The primary objective is to evaluate the efficacy and safety of XR-7491. The secondary objective is to evaluate the pharmacokinetic profile. The tertiary objective is to evaluate the quality of life. The study duration is 52 weeks. The follow-up duration is 12 weeks. The screening duration is 4 weeks. The treatment duration is 48 weeks. The safety duration is 60 weeks.

Appendix B: Informed Consent Form Excerpt
This study involves the administration of XR-7491. XR-7491 is an investigational drug. XR-7491 is not approved for marketing. XR-7491 is being studied for safety and efficacy. You may receive XR-7491 or a placebo. You may receive placebo. You may receive XR-7491. The assignment is random. The assignment is double-blind. The assignment is unknown to you. The assignment is unknown to the doctor.

Serum Potassium: 3.5 - 5.0 mmol/L
HbA1c: 4.0 - 5.6 %

Creatinine: 0.7 - 1.3 mg/dL
Blood Urea Nitrogen: 7 - 20 mg/dL
eGFR: 60 - 120 mL/min/1.73 m2

Appendix D: Adverse Event Grading Scale
Grade 1: Mild. Asymptomatic or mild symptoms. Clinical or diagnostic observations only. Intervention not indicated.
Grade 2: Moderate. Minimally symptomatic. Clinical or diagnostic observations only. Medical intervention indicated.
Grade 3: Severe. Markedly symptomatic. Limiting instrumental ADL. Hospitalization or prolongation of hospitalization indicated.
Grade 4: Life-threatening. Urgent intervention indicated.
Grade 5: Death.

Appendix E: Serious Adverse Event Criteria
1. Results in death.
2. Is life-threatening.
3. Requires inpatient hospitalization or prolongation of existing hospitalization.
4. Results in persistent or significant disability/incapacity.
5. Congenital anomaly/birth defect.
6. Other important medical events.

Appendix F: Dose Modification Guidelines
Dose Level 10mg: Standard dose.
Dose Level 5mg: Reduced dose.
Dose Level 0mg: Discontinued.
Dose Modification: Hold for Grade 3 AE.
Dose Modification: Reduce for Grade 2 AE.
Dose Modification: Resume for Grade 1 AE.
Dose Modification: Monitor for Grade 4 AE.
Dose Modification: Stop for Grade 5 AE.

Appendix G: Protocol Amendments

Amendment 2: Updated exclusion criteria.
Amendment 3: Updated safety reporting.
Amendment 4: Updated PK sampling.
Amendment 5: Updated DSMB charter.

AGENDA ITEM 2: EFFICACY REVIEW

DETAIL: "Liver function within normal limits."
DISCUSSION: ALT AND AST LEVELS WITHIN NORMAL LIMITS FOR ALL SUBJECTS. NO ELEVATIONS GREATER THAN 3 TIMES UPPER LIMIT OF NORMAL. HEMOGLOBIN AND PLATELET COUNTS STABLE. NO ANEMIA REPORTED. NO THROMBOCYTOPENIA REPORTED.
VOTE: UNANIMOUS IN FAVOR OF CONTINUATION.

{"ref_id": "clinical_trial_10_dx_oncology_biomarker_007", "text": "ONC-204 PHASE IIb CLINICAL TRIAL DATA BUNDLE
MONITORING SNAPSHOT: CYCLE 8 INTERIM REVIEW
SITE: NATIONAL CANCER CENTER
PRINCIPAL INVESTIGATOR: DR. T. BROWN
DATE OF REPORT GENERATION: 2023-08-12
STUDY DRUG: XR-7491 (ORAL JAK INHIBITOR PROTOCOL DESIGNATION ADJUSTED FOR ONC-204 INDICATION)
INDICATION: NON-SMALL CELL LUNG CANCER (NSCLC)
REGULATORY STATUS: ACTIVE PHASE IIb ENROLLMENT AND TREATMENT PHASE

PROTOCOL STATUS HEADER AND ENROLLMENT METRICS

The National Cancer Center site for the ONC-204 study has maintained active status throughout the monitoring period ending August 12, 2023. The study design remains consistent with the Phase IIb protocol specifications approved by the Institutional Review Board and the relevant regulatory authorities. The principal investigator, Dr. T. Brown, continues to oversee all clinical operations, patient safety monitoring, and data integrity checks at this location. The site has demonstrated adherence to the Good Clinical Practice (GCP) guidelines and the protocol-specific requirements for the management of Non-Small Cell Lung Cancer patients.

Enrollment metrics for the ONC-204 study at the National Cancer Center site are documented as follows. The total number of subjects screened for eligibility criteria prior to inclusion in the study population stands at 420. Screening activities included review of medical history, physical examination, performance status assessment (ECOG 0-2), and confirmation of Non-Small Cell Lung Cancer diagnosis via histological or cytological verification. Additional screening procedures involved mandatory biomarker testing, including PD-L1 expression analysis and circulating tumor DNA (ctDNA) quantification, to ensure subject stratification aligned with the study's inclusion criteria.

Of the 420 subjects initially screened, 115 subjects met all inclusion criteria and were formally enrolled into the ONC-204 study. Enrollment was conducted sequentially, with informed consent obtained prior to any study-related procedures. All enrolled subjects underwent a baseline assessment period to establish pre-treatment metrics, including laboratory values, radiographic imaging, and performance status.

Following enrollment, 112 subjects proceeded to the randomization phase of the study. The randomization process was performed using a centralized interactive web response system (IWRS) to ensure allocation concealment. The randomization ratio for the Phase IIb design was maintained at 1:1 between the active treatment arm and the control arm, as specified in the statistical analysis plan. The three subjects who were enrolled but not randomized were excluded from the randomization analysis set due to protocol deviations identified prior to the randomization window, specifically related to the timing of the first dose administration relative to the screening window. These subjects were managed according to the protocol-specified withdrawal procedures.

The current active treatment population consists of 112 randomized subjects. All randomized subjects have been assigned to a specific treatment arm and are receiving study medication in accordance with the dosing schedule. The site has maintained a high standard of documentation for all randomized subjects, with complete case report forms (CRFs) submitted for the majority of study visits. The enrollment rate has remained stable, with no significant deviations from the projected enrollment timeline for the Phase IIb stage. The site has reported no issues regarding subject recruitment capacity or retention rates during the current monitoring period.

The principal investigator, Dr. T. Brown, has confirmed that all study personnel involved in the ONC-204 trial have completed the required training modules, including GCP training, protocol-specific training, and emergency response procedures. The site has also maintained up-to-date investigator brochures and regulatory correspondence files. Regular monitoring visits have been conducted by the clinical research associates (CRAs) to verify source data, ensure compliance with the protocol, and assess the safety of the study participants. No critical findings or major deviations have been reported that would impact the integrity of the data or the safety of the subjects.

The National Cancer Center site continues to serve as a key enrollment hub for the ONC-204 study. The site's performance metrics indicate a robust operational capacity to manage the complexities of a Phase IIb trial in the setting of Non-Small Cell Lung Cancer. The site has successfully navigated the logistical requirements of coordinating multiple treatment arms, managing adverse events, and conducting the necessary biomarker analyses. The data generated at this site is considered reliable and suitable for inclusion in the final study database for regulatory submission.

ADVERSE EVENT REPORTS AND SAFETY MONITORING LOGS

The safety monitoring component of the ONC-204 Phase IIb study at the National Cancer Center site has been conducted in accordance with the protocol-specified safety reporting requirements. Adverse events (AEs) were defined as any untoward medical occurrence in a subject or clinical investigation subject administered a pharmaceutical product, which did not necessarily have to have a causal relationship with this treatment. All adverse events were collected from the time of the first dose of study medication until the final follow-up visit or study completion.

Two specific adverse event reports have been documented for the current monitoring snapshot period. These reports represent the most recent safety updates for the subjects under active treatment at the National Cancer Center site. The details of these reports are provided below with full adherence to MedDRA coding standards and protocol-specific severity grading criteria.

Adverse Event Report AE-204-013:
Subject ID: S-1220
Onset Date: 2023-08-01
Severity: Grade 1
Coded Term: Headache (MedDRA Preferred Term)
Relationship Assessment: Unlikely

The subject identified as S-1220 presented with an adverse event characterized by headache on August 1, 2023. The event was classified as Grade 1 according to the National Cancer Institute Common Terminology Criteria for Adverse Events (NCI CTCAE) version 5.0. Grade 1 severity indicates that the event was mild, asymptomatic, or mild symptoms, and clinical or diagnostic observations were indicated, but intervention was not required. The subject reported the headache during a routine follow-up visit. The onset of the headache was noted to be mild in intensity, described by the subject as a tension-type sensation without associated neurological deficits, visual disturbances, or nausea.

The relationship assessment for this adverse event was determined to be "Unlikely" in relation to the study drug XR-7491. This assessment was based on the temporal relationship between the dosing of the study medication and the onset of the symptom, as well as the known safety profile of the drug. The headache occurred several days after the administration of the previous cycle dose, and the subject had a history of occasional tension headaches prior to enrollment in the study. The medical monitor reviewed the case and determined that the event was consistent with the subject's baseline health status and did not warrant any specific intervention beyond standard supportive care. The subject continued to receive the study medication without interruption, and the headache resolved spontaneously within 48 hours without the need for pharmacological intervention.

Adverse Event Report AE-204-014:
Subject ID: S-1235
Onset Date: 2023-08-05
Severity: Grade 2
Coded Term: Erythema (MedDRA Preferred Term)
Relationship Assessment: Related

The subject identified as S-1235 presented with an adverse event characterized by erythema on August 5, 2023. The event was classified as Grade 2 according to the NCI CTCAE version 5.0. Grade 2 severity indicates that the event was moderate and required minimal, local, or non-invasive intervention. The subject reported the appearance of erythema on the upper extremities during a scheduled clinic visit. The erythema was observed to be localized to the forearms and hands, with mild redness and no associated blistering or ulceration. The subject described the sensation as mild itching but denied any pain or systemic symptoms such as fever or chills.

The relationship assessment for this adverse event was determined to be "Related" to the study drug XR-7491. This determination was based on the temporal association between the administration of the study medication and the onset of the skin reaction, as well as the known dermatological side effects associated with JAK inhibitors. The onset of the erythema occurred within the expected timeframe for such reactions following the administration of the cycle 8 dose. The medical monitor reviewed the case and confirmed that the event was consistent with a known safety signal for the drug class.

Management of the adverse event involved the initiation of topical corticosteroid therapy and the administration of antihistamines as per the protocol-specified guidelines for Grade 2 dermatological events. The subject was instructed to avoid direct sun exposure and to apply the topical medication as directed. The subject was monitored closely for progression of the reaction or the development of additional symptoms. The study medication was held temporarily for 48 hours pending the resolution of the erythema. Upon confirmation that the erythema had improved to Grade 1 or resolved, the subject was permitted to resume the study medication at the same dose level. No dose reductions were required for this subject. The event was reported to the Data Safety Monitoring Board (DSMB) and the regulatory authorities in accordance with the safety reporting timelines.

Both adverse events have been fully documented in the subject case report forms and the central safety database. The investigators have reviewed all new adverse events for causality, severity, and required actions. No serious adverse events (SAEs) or unexpected serious adverse reactions (USARs) have been reported for these subjects during the current monitoring period. The overall safety profile of the study drug at the National Cancer Center site remains consistent with the data observed in earlier phases of the ONC-204 trial.

LABORATORY PANELS AND BIOMARKER ANALYSIS

Comprehensive laboratory testing was performed on subjects S-1220 and S-1235 on July 30, 2023, as part of the Cycle 8 monitoring schedule. The laboratory panels included standard hematology and chemistry tests, as well as specific biomarker assays required for the stratification and efficacy analysis of the ONC-204 study. All laboratory results were generated using validated assays at the central reference laboratory accredited for the handling of oncology specimens.

Laboratory Panel for Subject S-1220:
Test Date: 2023-07-30
PD-L1 Expression: 95%
ctDNA Copy Number: 5 copies/mL
TSH: 1.6 mIU/L

Subject S-1220 underwent a comprehensive biomarker assessment on July 30, 2023. The PD-L1 expression level was determined to be 95%, indicating a high level of programmed death-ligand 1 expression on tumor cells. This value was derived from immunohistochemistry (IHC) staining of the tumor biopsy specimen, with the percentage representing the proportion of tumor cells showing membranous staining of any intensity. The high PD-L1 expression level is consistent with the subject's baseline characteristics and aligns with the inclusion criteria for the high-expression cohort in the Phase IIb study.

The circulating tumor DNA (ctDNA) copy number for subject S-1220 was measured at 5 copies/mL. This value was obtained via next-generation sequencing (NGS) of plasma samples. The ctDNA copy number reflects the burden of tumor-derived genetic material circulating in the blood. A level of 5 copies/mL indicates a low circulating tumor burden at the time of sampling. This finding is consistent with the subject's clinical response and supports the efficacy of the study drug in reducing tumor load. The ctDNA analysis was performed using a validated digital PCR assay, and the results were confirmed by a second independent run to ensure accuracy.

The Thyroid Stimulating Hormone (TSH) level for subject S-1220 was recorded as 1.6 mIU/L. This value falls within the standard reference range of 0.4 to 4.0 mIU/L. The TSH level indicates normal thyroid function, with no evidence of hypothyroidism or hyperthyroidism. This is a relevant parameter given the potential for immune-mediated endocrine adverse events associated with JAK inhibitors. The normal TSH level suggests that the subject's thyroid function remains stable and is not adversely affected by the study medication.

Laboratory Panel for Subject S-1235:
Test Date: 2023-07-30
PD-L1 Expression: 35%
ctDNA Copy Number: 110 copies/mL
TSH: 2.5 mIU/L

Subject S-1235 underwent a comprehensive biomarker assessment on July 30, 2023, concurrent with subject S-1220. The PD-L1 expression level for this subject was determined to be 35%. This value was also derived from IHC staining of the tumor biopsy specimen. The 35% expression level indicates a moderate level of PD-L1 expression, which is consistent with the subject's stratification in the study. The subject falls within the intermediate expression cohort, which is a key subgroup for the efficacy analysis of the ONC-204 trial.

The circulating tumor DNA (ctDNA) copy number for subject S-1235 was measured at 110 copies/mL. This value was obtained via the same validated NGS assay used for subject S-1220. A level of 110 copies/mL indicates a higher circulating tumor burden compared to subject S-1220. This finding is consistent with the subject's baseline characteristics and suggests a more active disease state at the time of sampling. The elevated ctDNA level is an important marker for monitoring treatment response and may influence the assessment of efficacy in this subject. The results were verified through a second independent run to ensure the accuracy of the high copy number measurement.

The Thyroid Stimulating Hormone (TSH) level for subject S-1235 was recorded as 2.5 mIU/L. This value falls within the standard reference range of 0.4 to 4.0 mIU/L. The TSH level indicates normal thyroid function for this subject as well. The stability of the TSH level suggests that the subject's thyroid function is not compromised by the study medication. This is a positive indicator of the safety profile of the drug in this patient population.

In addition to the specific biomarkers listed above, standard laboratory panels including complete blood count (CBC) with differential, comprehensive metabolic panel (CMP), and liver function tests (LFTs) were performed on both subjects. All values for CBC, CMP, and LFTs were within normal limits or showed no clinically significant deviations from baseline. No signs of hepatotoxicity, hematologic toxicity, or metabolic abnormalities were observed in the laboratory results for either subject. The laboratory data supports the continued administration of the study medication to both subjects.

The biomarker data collected on July 30, 2023, will be integrated into the final efficacy analysis of the ONC-204 study. The correlation between PD-L1 expression levels, ctDNA copy numbers, and clinical response will be evaluated to determine the predictive value of these biomarkers in the treatment of Non-Small Cell Lung Cancer. The data from these two subjects contribute to the overall dataset used for stratification and subgroup analysis.

DOSING LOGS AND MEDICATION ADMINISTRATION RECORDS

The dosing logs for the ONC-204 study at the National Cancer Center site have been meticulously maintained to ensure accurate tracking of study medication administration. The logs document the dose level, cycle number, and compliance percentage for each subject receiving the study drug. The dosing schedule for the Phase IIb study is defined as 200 mg IV Q3W (intravenous administration every three weeks). This regimen is consistent with the protocol specifications for the treatment of Non-Small Cell Lung Cancer.

Dosing Log for Subject S-1220:
Subject ID: S-1220
Dose Level: 200 mg IV Q3W
Cycle: 8
Compliance Percent: 100

Subject S-1220 is currently in Cycle 8 of the ONC-204 study. The subject has received the full prescribed dose of 200 mg of the study medication via intravenous infusion every three weeks. The administration of the study drug was performed in the outpatient infusion center under the supervision of qualified nursing staff. The infusion duration was monitored to ensure the medication was administered over the specified time frame. The subject tolerated the infusion well, with no immediate adverse reactions observed during the administration period.

The compliance percentage for subject S-1220 is recorded as 100%. This indicates that the subject has adhered strictly to the dosing schedule, receiving all scheduled doses without any missed administrations or dose interruptions. The subject has been compliant with the protocol requirements for medication intake and has attended all scheduled clinic visits for dosing and monitoring. The high compliance rate is a positive indicator of the subject's engagement in the study and the reliability of the efficacy data generated from this subject.

Dosing Log for Subject S-1235:
Subject ID: S-1235
Dose Level: 200 mg IV Q3W
Cycle: 8
Compliance Percent: 95

Subject S-1235 is also currently in Cycle 8 of the ONC-204 study. The subject has received the full prescribed dose of 200 mg of the study medication via intravenous infusion every three weeks. The administration of the study drug was performed in the outpatient infusion center under the supervision of qualified nursing staff. The infusion duration was monitored to ensure the medication was administered over the specified time frame. The subject tolerated the infusion well, with no immediate adverse reactions observed during the administration period, aside from the previously noted erythema which was managed as an adverse event.

The compliance percentage for subject S-1235 is recorded as 95%. This indicates a high level of adherence to the dosing schedule, with a minor deviation from 100% compliance. The slight reduction in compliance is attributed to a temporary hold of the study medication for 48 hours due to the Grade 2 erythema adverse event. The hold was lifted once the adverse event resolved to Grade 1, and the subject resumed the dosing schedule. The subject has otherwise adhered to the protocol requirements for medication intake and has attended all scheduled clinic visits for dosing and monitoring. The compliance rate remains high, and the temporary hold was managed in accordance with the protocol-specified guidelines for adverse event management.

The dosing logs for both subjects confirm that the study medication has been administered according to the protocol specifications. The dose level of 200 mg IV Q3W has been maintained throughout the treatment period for both subjects. The compliance percentages reflect the subjects' adherence to the study regimen and provide confidence in the interpretation of the efficacy and safety data. The dosing logs are an integral part of the data bundle and are used to correlate medication exposure with clinical outcomes and laboratory findings.

PHARMACOKINETIC SUMMARIES AND DRUG EXPOSURE ANALYSIS

Pharmacokinetic (PK) data were collected for subject S-1220 during Cycle 8 of the ONC-204 study to assess the drug exposure and disposition of the study medication. The PK analysis is a critical component of the Phase IIb study, providing insights into the absorption, distribution, metabolism, and excretion of the drug in the target population. The PK parameters were calculated using non-compartmental analysis methods and are reported with standard statistical precision.

Pharmacokinetic Summary for Subject S-1220:
Subject ID: S-1220
Cmax (Maximum Concentration): 152.0 ng/mL
Tmax (Time to Maximum Concentration): 1.9 hours
Half-Life (t1/2): 19.2 hours

The maximum plasma concentration (Cmax) for subject S-1220 was determined to be 152.0 ng/mL. This value represents the peak concentration of the study drug in the plasma following the intravenous administration of the 200 mg dose. The Cmax value is consistent with the expected exposure levels for the drug in this patient population and falls within the range observed in previous cycles and earlier phases of the study. The high Cmax indicates adequate drug exposure at the time of administration.

The time to maximum concentration (Tmax) for subject S-1220 was recorded as 1.9 hours. This parameter reflects the time required for the drug to reach its peak concentration in the plasma after administration. The Tmax value of 1.9 hours is consistent with the rapid distribution phase of the drug following intravenous infusion. The quick attainment of peak concentration is typical for intravenous administration and supports the dosing schedule of every three weeks.

The half-life (t1/2) for subject S-1220 was calculated to be 19.2 hours. This parameter indicates the time required for the plasma concentration of the drug to decrease by 50% during the elimination phase. The half-life of 19.2 hours suggests that the drug is eliminated from the body relatively quickly, supporting the dosing interval of every three weeks. The half-life value is consistent with the pharmacokinetic profile observed in other subjects in the study and aligns with the known properties of the drug class.

The pharmacokinetic data for subject S-1220 indicates that the drug exposure is within the therapeutic range and that the dosing regimen is appropriate for maintaining effective drug levels throughout the treatment cycle. The PK parameters provide a quantitative basis for understanding the drug's behavior in the body and support the safety and efficacy findings of the study. The data will be used to refine the dosing recommendations for future phases of the ONC-204 trial.

DATA SAFETY MONITORING BOARD (DSMB) MINUTES AND RECOMMENDATIONS

The Data Safety Monitoring Board (DSMB) for the ONC-204 Phase IIb study convened a scheduled meeting on August 10, 2023. The meeting was attended by all DSMB members, including independent statisticians, clinicians, and patient advocates. The purpose of the meeting was to review the interim safety and efficacy data for the study, assess the risk-benefit profile of the study drug, and provide recommendations regarding the continuation of the trial.

DSMB Meeting Details:
Meeting Date: 2023-08-10
Recommendation: Approve for Phase III
Safety Update: Final safety review complete. Biomarker stratification successful. Risk-benefit profile favorable for registration.

The DSMB conducted a comprehensive review of all data available up to the meeting date, including adverse event reports, laboratory results, dosing logs, and pharmacokinetic summaries. The board examined the safety data for all randomized subjects at the National Cancer Center site and other participating sites. The review included an analysis of the frequency and severity of adverse events, the incidence of serious adverse events, and the relationship of these events to the study drug.

The safety update presented to the DSMB indicated that the final safety review was complete. The board noted that the safety profile of the study drug was consistent with the known safety profile of JAK inhibitors and the data observed in earlier phases of the ONC-204 trial. No new safety signals or unexpected adverse events were identified during the review. The adverse events reported at the National Cancer Center site, including the headache and erythema, were deemed manageable and consistent with the expected safety profile.

The DSMB also reviewed the biomarker stratification data. The board confirmed that the stratification based on PD-L1 expression and ctDNA copy number was successful. The analysis demonstrated that the biomarkers were predictive of treatment response and that the stratification strategy was effective in identifying patients who would benefit most from the study drug. The successful biomarker stratification supports the use of these markers in the design of future trials and for patient selection in clinical practice.

Based on the comprehensive review of the safety and efficacy data, the DSMB concluded that the risk-benefit profile of the study drug was favorable for registration. The board determined that the benefits of the study drug in the treatment of Non-Small Cell Lung Cancer outweighed the risks associated with its use. The DSMB recommended that the study proceed to Phase III, with the goal of conducting a large-scale, confirmatory trial to support regulatory approval.

The recommendation to approve for Phase III was made unanimously by the DSMB. The board emphasized the importance of continuing the study to gather additional data on long-term efficacy and safety. The DSMB also recommended that the Phase III trial incorporate the biomarker stratification strategy used in the Phase IIb study to further refine patient selection and optimize treatment outcomes.

The DSMB minutes and recommendations will be documented in the study file and submitted to the regulatory authorities as part of the regulatory submission package. The board's decision to recommend Phase III is a significant milestone for the ONC-204 study and represents a major step forward in the development of the study drug for the treatment of Non-Small Cell Lung Cancer.

STUDY OPERATIONS AND ADMINISTRATIVE DETAILS

The operational aspects of the ONC-204 Phase IIb study at the National Cancer Center site have been managed with a high degree of efficiency and adherence to protocol requirements. The site has maintained a robust infrastructure for patient care, data collection, and safety monitoring. The principal investigator, Dr. T. Brown, has provided strong leadership and oversight to ensure the successful execution of the study.

The site has established a dedicated study team comprising physicians, nurses, research coordinators, and pharmacists. All team members have received comprehensive training on the study protocol, GCP guidelines, and the specific requirements of the ONC-204 trial. The team has demonstrated a high level of competence and dedication to the study objectives. The coordination of patient visits, dosing administration, and laboratory sample collection has been seamless, minimizing delays and ensuring the timely collection of data.

The site has also maintained open communication channels with the sponsor and the DSMB. Regular updates have been provided on the status of the study, including enrollment progress, safety data, and operational challenges. The site has responded promptly to any queries or requests for information from the sponsor or regulatory authorities. The transparency and responsiveness of the site have contributed to the smooth progress of the study.

Administrative tasks such as regulatory filing, ethics committee reporting, and contract management have been handled efficiently. The site has maintained up-to-date regulatory documentation and has ensured compliance with all applicable laws and regulations. The site has also demonstrated a commitment to data quality, with rigorous checks and balances in place to ensure the accuracy and integrity of the data collected.

The site has also engaged in proactive risk management, identifying potential issues before they became problems. For example, the site anticipated potential bottlenecks in the biomarker testing process and implemented contingency plans to ensure timely results. The proactive approach to risk management has helped to maintain the study timeline and ensure the quality of the data.

The National Cancer Center site has served as a model for other sites participating in the ONC-204 study. The site's performance has been recognized by the sponsor and the DSMB for its excellence in clinical trial management. The site's contributions to the study have been invaluable, and the data generated at this site will play a critical role in the evaluation of the study drug.

The site has also maintained a strong focus on patient safety and welfare. The study team has demonstrated a deep commitment to the well-being of the subjects, ensuring that all procedures are conducted with care and respect. The site has provided comprehensive support to the subjects, including counseling and resources to help them manage the challenges of the study. The patient-centric approach of the site has contributed to the high retention rate and the positive experience of the subjects.

The site has also engaged in educational activities to disseminate the knowledge gained from the ONC-204 study. The principal investigator and study team have presented findings at national and international conferences, contributing to the broader scientific community's understanding of the treatment of Non-Small Cell Lung Cancer. The site's commitment to knowledge sharing has enhanced the reputation of the ONC-204 study and has facilitated the translation of research findings into clinical practice.

The operational success of the ONC-204 study at the National Cancer Center site is a testament to the hard work and dedication of the entire study team. The site has exceeded expectations in all aspects of study conduct, from patient recruitment to data analysis. CONCLUSION AND DATA INTEGRITY STATEMENT

SECTION 1: PROTOCOL STATUS HEADER AND STUDY OVERVIEW

This document serves as a comprehensive monitoring snapshot for the XR-7491 Phase IIb clinical trial. The study utilizes an oral JAK inhibitor designated as XR-7491 for the treatment of rheumatoid arthritis. The current phase of the study is designated as escalation. The data contained within this bundle reflects the status of the trial as of the date of issue, 2025-04-17. All data entries correspond to the active protocol version 2.0. This version of the protocol was active at the time of data collection and reporting.

The study involves multiple sites. The primary focus of this snapshot is the data generated from Site-016. This site has been approved for activation. Site activation approved status is recorded in the central registry. The Principal Investigator for this site is PI_Thompson. The PI is responsible for the oversight of all clinical activities at this location. The site ID is recorded as SITE-016 in the master site list. The enrollment status for this site is currently Open. Open status indicates that subject recruitment is active and ongoing.

The study design includes a double-blind, placebo-controlled structure. Blinding maintained status is confirmed for all arms. The blinding code is held centrally by the Sponsor. The Sponsor Rep is the designated contact for data queries and protocol deviations. The DSMB Chair oversees the safety review process. The Safety Monitor is responsible for the daily oversight of adverse event reporting. The Efficacy Committee is responsible for the interim analysis of efficacy metrics.

The total number of subjects enrolled across all sites at the time of this report is 130. Total subjects enrolled: 130. This figure includes subjects from Site-016 and all other participating centers. The escalation phase involves dose titration to determine the optimal therapeutic window. The dose levels under investigation include 5mg, 15mg, and 30mg. A placebo arm is also included in the randomization scheme. The randomization code is ARM-03 for the 30mg arm. The randomization code is ARM-01 for the placebo arm. The randomization code is ARM-02 for the 5mg arm. The randomization code is ARM-04 for the 15mg arm.

The protocol version 2.0 active status is noted in the header. This version supersedes the previous version 1.0. Any amendments to the protocol are tracked in the amendment log. The amendment log is reviewed by the DSMB. The DSMB notes are documented in the meeting minutes. The meeting ID for the most recent review is DSMB-013. The meeting date is 2025-04-17. The attendees for this meeting include the DSMB Chair and the Sponsor Rep.

The data cutoff for this report is 2025-04-16. Data cutoff: 2025-04-16. All data collected prior to this date is included in the analysis. Data collected after this date is reserved for the next reporting cycle. The next review is scheduled for Week 14. Next review scheduled: Week 14. This schedule ensures regular monitoring of safety and efficacy signals. The monitoring frequency is consistent with the Phase IIb escalation requirements.

The study follows Good Clinical Practice (GCP) guidelines. All personnel have completed required training. Site staff training up to date status is recorded in the training log. The Investigational Product account is reconciled regularly. Investigational product account reconciled status is confirmed in the pharmacy records. The Chain of custody maintained status applies to all drug shipments. Chain of custody maintained ensures the integrity of the medication from manufacture to administration.

PAGE 2 OF 18

question.prompt: Is there sufficient evidence to recommend suspending the 30mg dose arm?

ground_truth.canonical_answer: Yes. Four subjects have ALT above ULN, one required dose reduction at 3.1×ULN, and the trajectory is accelerating. The dose-dependence is clear. The mid-dose arm is lagging by ~4 weeks on the same curve. Continuing the 30mg arm risks Hy's Law cases.
key_facts: ALT elevations in the XR-7491 trial are dose-dependent with the 30mg arm showing the clearest trajectory
the 15mg mid-dose arm is now showing the same ALT trajectory the 30mg arm showed 4 weeks earlier
the hepatotoxicity signal has a 4-6 week lag from dose initiation making early monitoring reviews appear clean

answer.answer_text:
Based on the provided clinical trial data, there is **not sufficient evidence to recommend suspending the 30mg dose arm** at this time. While there are safety signals indicating liver enzyme elevations, the Data Safety Monitoring Board (DSMB) has explicitly recommended against suspension, opting instead for enhanced monitoring.

The specific evidence supporting this conclusion includes:

**1. DSMB Recommendation to Continue with Enhanced Monitoring**
The Data Safety Monitoring Board (DSMB) reviewed the safety data and unanimously voted to continue the study. They concluded that the risk-benefit ratio remains favorable and that the safety profile is acceptable, provided that monitoring is intensified.
*   The DSMB concluded that "the study can continue with enhanced monitoring" and that the "safety profile is acceptable" [clinical_trial_10_ep_014].
*   The DSMB vote on study continuation passed unanimously, and the vote on suspension was explicitly "Passed (Unanimous)" in the context of *not* suspending, but rather continuing with conditions [clinical_trial_10_ep_014].
*   The specific recommendation was to increase the frequency of liver function testing for subjects in the 30mg arm [clinical_trial_10_ep_014].

**2. Nature of Safety Signals (Liver Enzyme Elevations)**
The primary concern driving the safety review is the observation of elevated liver enzymes (ALT and AST) specifically in the 30mg arm, which was not observed in lower dose arms or placebo.
*   "Liver function trajectory diverging" was noted, with ALT and AST elevations observed in multiple subjects in the 30mg arm [clinical_trial_10_ep_014].
*   Earlier data indicated ALT elevations in 30mg arm subjects with values of 58, 62, and 65 U/L (Reference range 7-56 U/L), whereas no elevations were seen in the placebo, 5mg, or 15mg arms [clinical_trial_10_ep_012_c28].
*   However, the DSMB assessment confirmed that "No elevations exceed 3x ULN" for ALT or AST, and there is "No evidence of liver failure," "No evidence of drug-induced liver injury (DILI)," or "No evidence of acute hepatitis" [clinical_trial_10_ep_014].

**3. Lack of Serious Adverse Events (SAEs) or Discontinuations**
Despite the liver enzyme elevations, there have been no severe clinical consequences requiring the arm to be stopped.
*   There have been **0 Serious Adverse Events (SAEs)** and **0 deaths** reported in the current reporting period [clinical_trial_10_ep_014].
*   There have been **0 study discontinuations due to adverse events** [clinical_trial_10_ep_014].
*   All reported adverse events in the 30mg arm were classified as "Minor" [clinical_trial_10_ep_014].

**4. Efficacy Benefits**
The 30mg arm demonstrates the highest efficacy, which supports the risk-benefit analysis favoring continuation.
*   The 30mg arm showed the highest ACR20 response rate (80%) and the greatest DAS28 score reduction (2.6 points) compared to placebo (54% response, 1.1 point reduction) [clinical_trial_10_ep_014].
*   A dose-response relationship was confirmed, with the 30mg arm showing the most significant efficacy separation [clinical_trial_10_ep_014].

**Conclusion**
While the 30mg arm exhibits a dose-dependent divergence in liver function markers (ALT/AST elevations), the elevations are currently mild (below 3x ULN), not associated with serious adverse events or discontinuations, and the arm provides the highest efficacy. Consequently, the DSMB has determined that **enhanced monitoring** (weekly liver function tests) is the appropriate action rather than suspension [clinical_trial_10_ep_014, clinical_trial_10_ep_012_c28].

ENROLLMENT LOG DETAILS:
The enrollment log for SITE-014, recorded on 2025-04-11, indicates active recruitment and baseline assessment procedures are ongoing. The site activation was approved in accordance with regulatory requirements and internal SOPs. The protocol version 2.0 is currently active and in use for all subject interactions at this location.

Subject_028 was enrolled on 2025-04-11. Subject_028 is a female, 53 years of age. Subject_029 was also enrolled on 2025-04-11. Subject_029 is a male, 58 years of age. Baseline labs were drawn for both subjects on the date of enrollment. Informed consent was obtained for both subjects in compliance with Good Clinical Practice (GCP) guidelines. Randomization was completed for 110 subjects across the study population prior to the current reporting window.

Subject_028 was assigned to the 30mg dose level. The randomization code for Subject_028 is ARM-03 (30mg). Subject_029 was assigned to the 30mg dose level. The randomization code for Subject_029 is ARM-03 (30mg). Drug administration was performed for both subjects immediately following the baseline assessment and randomization process. The Investigational Product (IP) was dispensed in accordance with the dosing schedule defined in the protocol.

Randomization code: ARM-03 (30mg).
Informed consent obtained.

The total number of subjects enrolled in the study as of the data cutoff date of 2025-04-10 is 115. This figure includes all subjects enrolled across all participating sites. The enrollment status remains Open. No site closures or suspensions have been reported for the current reporting period. The enrollment trajectory is consistent with the projected timeline outlined in the master protocol.

SITE-014 is currently operating under standard operating procedures for subject management. PI_Harris has oversight of all clinical activities at the site. The site staff has completed all required training modules relevant to the current protocol version. The site is maintaining a current master file and essential documents are up to date.

Enrollment details for the current cohort:
Cohort 1 (Placebo): 28 subjects enrolled.
Cohort 2 (5mg): 29 subjects enrolled.
Cohort 3 (15mg): 30 subjects enrolled.
Cohort 4 (30mg): 28 subjects enrolled.
Total enrolled: 115 subjects.

Randomization allocation ratio: 1:1:1:1.
Blinding maintenance procedures are in place.
Blinding maintained.

The site has reported no significant deviations from the protocol regarding enrollment procedures. All consent forms are signed and dated. The subject identification codes are stored securely in the electronic data capture (EDC) system. The chain of custody for biological samples is documented.

Baseline assessment protocols were followed for Subject_028 and Subject_029. Vital signs were recorded. Physical examinations were conducted. Medical history was updated. Concomitant medications were documented. The baseline disease activity scores were recorded for both subjects.

The enrollment log for 2025-04-11 reflects routine administrative processing. No delays in subject screening or enrollment were noted. The site capacity remains sufficient to handle the current enrollment rate. The site is in good standing with the sponsor.

STUDY IDENTIFICATION: XR-7491-2025-001
PHASE: red_herring / Phase IIb
INDICATION: Rheumatoid Arthritis
INTERVENTIONAL AGENT: XR-7491 (Oral JAK Inhibitor)
SPONSOR: XR Pharma Inc.
STUDY PROTOCOL: Version 2.0
DATE OF REPORT: 2025-03-27
REPORT TYPE: Monitoring Snapshot
DOCUMENT ID: BUNDLE-2025-03-27
PAGE COUNT: 42
CONFIDENTIALITY LEVEL: RESTRICTED
REGULATORY STATUS: ACTIVE

1. PROTOCOL STATUS HEADER AND ADMINISTRATIVE OVERVIEW

1.1 Study Identification and Governance
The clinical trial data bundle for the Phase IIb study of XR-7491, designated internally as red_herring for the monitoring period ending 2025-03-27, is hereby submitted for review. This document constitutes a routine snapshot of the monitoring data associated with the investigational product XR-7491. The study is conducted in accordance with the Declaration of Helsinki, ICH-GCP guidelines, and local regulatory requirements. The protocol version currently active for all sites is Protocol Version 2.0. This version was approved by the Institutional Review Board (IRB) and the Independent Ethics Committee (IEC) on 2024-11-15. The study sponsor, XR Pharma Inc., retains full responsibility for the conduct of the trial and the integrity of the data.

1.2 Site Information and Principal Investigator
The primary data submission originates from Site-009. The site is located in a tertiary care center specializing in rheumatology. The Principal Investigator for Site-009 is PI_Taylor. PI_Taylor holds a current license to practice medicine and has completed the required Good Clinical Practice (GCP) training modules. The site activation was approved by the sponsor on 2025-01-10. The site has been operational for 12 weeks as of the data cutoff date. The site contact information is on file in the Investigator Site File (ISF) under document code SITE-009-CON. The site manager is responsible for daily operations and staff supervision. Staff turnover is within acceptable limits. All clinical staff have signed delegation of authority logs. The site maintains a secure facility for the storage of investigational products and source documents. The facility meets the requirements for controlled substance storage where applicable.

1.3 Enrollment Status and Randomization
The enrollment status for the reporting period is Open. The site is actively recruiting subjects meeting the inclusion and exclusion criteria. The total subjects enrolled across all sites is 78. At Site-009, randomization has been completed for 75 subjects. The randomization code for the most recent subjects is ARM-04 (Placebo). Randomization was performed using an Interactive Web Response System (IWRS). The IWRS is managed by the sponsor's data management team. The randomization schedule is stratified by disease duration and prior biologic use. The allocation ratio is 1:1:1:1 across the four treatment arms. The treatment arms include Placebo, 5mg XR-7491, 15mg XR-7491, and 30mg XR-7491. Blinding is maintained throughout the study period. Unblinding procedures are available only for medical emergencies. The study is double-blind. Neither the investigator nor the subject knows the treatment assignment. The code break envelope is sealed and stored in the ISF.

1.4 Protocol Version and Amendments
Protocol Version 2.0 is active. This version superseded Version 1.0 which was used during the pilot phase. The amendment was implemented to adjust the dosing schedule. The amendment was submitted to the regulatory authorities on 2025-02-01. The implementation date was 2025-02-15. All subjects enrolled prior to the amendment are grandfathered under the original protocol provisions unless they meet the new criteria. The new criteria apply to subjects enrolled on or after 2025-02-15. This ensures consistency in the safety profile analysis. The sponsor has notified all sites of the change. Site-009 has updated the subject consent forms to reflect the new protocol version. The informed consent obtained for each subject references the current version of the protocol. The consent form includes a description of the investigational product and the potential risks.

2. ADVERSE EVENT REPORTS

2.1 Adverse Event Collection Methodology
Adverse events are collected from the date of informed consent until the end of the study or 30 days after the last dose of investigational product. The collection method involves daily monitoring by the study nurse and weekly review by the Principal Investigator. All adverse events are recorded in the Case Report Forms (CRFs). The MedDRA coding dictionary version 28.0 is used for adverse event coding. The coding is performed by the sponsor's medical writing team. The coding is verified by a medical monitor. Adverse events are categorized by system organ class and preferred term. The severity is graded according to the National Cancer Institute Common Terminology Criteria for Adverse Events (CTCAE) version 5.0. The relationship to the investigational product is assessed using the Naranjo Scale or sponsor-specific criteria.

2.2 Adverse Event Summary Table
The following table summarizes the adverse events reported during the monitoring period. The data cutoff for this section is 2025-03-26. The total number of subjects enrolled is 78. The total number of adverse events recorded is 16 minor. There are no serious adverse events reported. There are no adverse events of special interest reported. The adverse events are distributed across the treatment arms. The distribution is consistent with the known safety profile of JAK inhibitors. The events are non-specific. The events do not show a dose-response relationship. The events resolved without intervention in 14 cases. Two cases required medical intervention. The medical intervention did not result in discontinuation. The safety signal assessment is ongoing. The DSMB Review 6 notes no safety signals. This assessment is based on the aggregate data. The data review conducted by the DSMB was comprehensive. The review included all raw data and source documents. The Safety Monitor reviewed the data independently. The Safety Monitor is a qualified medical professional. The Efficacy Committee reviewed the data for consistency.
